items.verse: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَٱلَّذِينَ مَعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ وَأَغْرَقْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا عَمِينَ
items.verseEn: But they denied him, so We saved him and those who were with him in the ship. And We drowned those who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a blind people.

items.verse: فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَأَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلسَّفِينَةِ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهَآ ءَايَةًۭ لِّلْعَـٰلَمِينَ
items.verseEn: But We saved him and the companions of the ship, and We made it a sign for the worlds.

items.verse: إِنَّا لَمَّا طَغَا ٱلْمَآءُ حَمَلْنَـٰكُمْ فِى ٱلْجَارِيَةِ
items.verseEn: Indeed, when the water overflowed, We carried you [i.e., your ancestors] in the sailing ship
And We carried him on a [construction of] planks and nails,

items.verse: وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ
items.verseEn: But they who believe and do righteous deeds - those are the companions of Paradise; they will abide therein eternally.

items.verse: جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍۢ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ
items.verseEn: Gardens of perpetual residence beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally. And that is the reward of one who purifies himself.

items.verse: ٱلَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ ٱلْفِرْدَوْسَ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ
items.verseEn: Who will inherit al-Firdaus. They will abide therein eternally.
Near it is the Garden of Refuge [i.e., Paradise] -

categories.name: مُصْطَلَحَاتُ الْمُعَامَلَاتِ الْقُرْآنِيَّةِ
categories.nameEn: Quranic Terms for Contracts and Transactions

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا نُودِىَ لِلصَّلَوٰةِ مِن يَوْمِ ٱلْجُمُعَةِ فَٱسْعَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ وَذَرُوا۟ ٱلْبَيْعَ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌۭ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, when [the adhān] is called for the prayer on the day of Jumuʿah [Friday], then proceed to the remembrance of Allāh and leave trade. That is better for you, if you only knew.

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَأْكُلُوا۟ ٱلرِّبَوٰٓا۟ أَضْعَـٰفًۭا مُّضَـٰعَفَةًۭ ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, do not consume usury, doubled and multiplied, but fear Allāh that you may be successful.

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَوْفُوا۟ بِٱلْعُقُودِ ۚ أُحِلَّتْ لَكُم بَهِيمَةُ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ إِلَّا مَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ غَيْرَ مُحِلِّى ٱلصَّيْدِ وَأَنتُمْ حُرُمٌ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَحْكُمُ مَا يُرِيدُ
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, fulfill [all] contracts. Lawful for you are the animals of grazing livestock except for that which is recited to you [in this Qur’ān] - hunting not being permitted while you are in the state of iḥrām. Indeed, Allāh ordains what He intends.

items.verse: إِنَّا عَرَضْنَا ٱلْأَمَانَةَ عَلَى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱلْجِبَالِ فَأَبَيْنَ أَن يَحْمِلْنَهَا وَأَشْفَقْنَ مِنْهَا وَحَمَلَهَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ ظَلُومًۭا جَهُولًۭا
items.verseEn: Indeed, We offered the Trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, and they declined to bear it and feared it; but man [undertook to] bear it. Indeed, he was unjust and ignorant.

items.verse: قَالَ لَهُۥ صَاحِبُهُۥ وَهُوَ يُحَاوِرُهُۥٓ أَكَفَرْتَ بِٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكَ مِن تُرَابٍۢ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍۢ ثُمَّ سَوَّىٰكَ رَجُلًۭا
items.verseEn: His companion said to him while he was conversing with him, "Have you disbelieved in He who created you from dust and then from a sperm-drop and then proportioned you [as] a man?

items.verse: قَالَ مَا مَنَعَكَ أَلَّا تَسْجُدَ إِذْ أَمَرْتُكَ ۖ قَالَ أَنَا۠ خَيْرٌۭ مِّنْهُ خَلَقْتَنِى مِن نَّارٍۢ وَخَلَقْتَهُۥ مِن طِينٍۢ
items.verseEn: [Allāh] said, "What prevented you from prostrating when I commanded you?" [Satan] said, "I am better than him. You created me from fire and created him from clay [i.e., earth]."

items.verse: قَالَ لَمْ أَكُن لِّأَسْجُدَ لِبَشَرٍ خَلَقْتَهُۥ مِن صَلْصَـٰلٍۢ مِّنْ حَمَإٍۢ مَّسْنُونٍۢ
items.verseEn: He said, "Never would I prostrate to a human whom You created out of clay from an altered black mud."
He created man from clay like [that of] pottery.

harf.hint: Sarah's reaction when told she would have a child in her old age - expressing profound amazement
harf.arabicVerse: قَالَتْ يَـٰوَيْلَتَىٰٓ ءَأَلِدُ وَأَنَا۠ عَجُوزٌۭ وَهَـٰذَا بَعْلِى شَيْخًا ۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَشَىْءٌ عَجِيبٌۭ
harf.verse: 11:72 - She said, "Woe to me! Shall I give birth while I am an old woman and this, my husband, is an old man? Indeed, this is an amazing thing!"

deduction.title: The Flame That Bared a Legacy
deduction.intro: In the annals of resistance, there stands a figure whose very name became a warning unto the end of time. This person chose enmity over kinship, defiance over submission, and stood in opposition to a call of mercy. Listen carefully to the echoes of an ancient proclamation as we uncover the identity of this notorious soul.
clues: I was born into the same clan as the final messenger of Allah, yet I stood against them at every turn.
My name carried meaning—a name meaning 'father of flame,' a name that would foreshadow my eternal destiny.
I unleashed harm against the bearer of truth, and even my own household joined in this campaign of cruelty.
I used my wealth and influence to silence the call to Allah, believing that money could extinguish divine light.
Allah dedicated an entire chapter to me—a chapter so brief that it can be recited in seconds, yet its weight remains till the Day of Judgment.
My companion in the Hereafter walks behind me, bound by a rope of fiber around the neck, carrying the very thorns they used to spread.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Abu Lahab
Abu Jahl
Waraqah ibn Nawfal
Umayyah ibn Khalaf
Uqbah ibn Abi Mu'ayt

trial.label: Defining Confrontation
options: Leading the opposition against the Prophet in Mecca
Migrating to Abyssinia to escape persecution
Building the Kaaba with Prophet Ibrahim
Accepting Islam on the deathbed
Forging the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah
trial.answer: Leading the opposition against the Prophet in Mecca

location.label: Key Element
options: A wife who carried thorns and firewood
A staff that transformed into a serpent
A ring bearing the seal of Solomon
A golden calf worshipped by the people
A spring that gushed forth water
location.answer: A wife who carried thorns and firewood

outcome.label: Final Reckoning
options: An entire surah revealed declaring total ruin
Became the leader of the Muslim community
Received forgiveness at the moment of death
Built a great mosque in Medina
Was resurrected among the righteous
outcome.answer: An entire surah revealed declaring total ruin

deduction.arabic: مَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُ مَالُهُۥ وَمَا كَسَبَ
deduction.verse: His wealth will not avail him or that which he gained. (111:2)

translations: O you who believe O you who believe O you who believe When it is said
to you Make room in the assemblies then make room
Allah will make room Allah will make room for you And when
it is said Rise up then rise up Allah will raise
Allah will raise those who believe among you
and those who were given the knowledge (in) degrees
And Allah of what you do (is) All-Aware
scramble.hint: A divine promise about knowledge expansion and a call to supplicate for increased understanding
scramble.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمْ تَفَسَّحُوا۟ فِى ٱلْمَجَـٰلِسِ فَٱفْسَحُوا۟ يَفْسَحِ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۖ وَإِذَا قِيلَ ٱنشُزُوا۟ فَٱنشُزُوا۟ يَرْفَعِ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنكُمْ وَٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ دَرَجَـٰتٍۢ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌۭ
scramble.verseEn: O you who have believed, when you are told, "Space yourselves" in assemblies, then make space; Allāh will make space for you. And when you are told, "Arise," then arise; Allāh will raise those who have believed among you and those who were given knowledge, by degrees. And Allāh is Aware of what you do.
